message:
feat(inet): add inet_net_pton + inet_net_ntop (BIND CIDR codec) (bd-cvixn)

BIND/libresolv inet_net_pton(af, src, dst, size) parses CIDR-like
network-address strings ("192.168.0/24", "10/8", "192.168.0.1")
into the dst byte buffer, returning the prefix length in bits.
Companion inet_net_ntop(af, src, bits, dst, size) renders network
bytes + prefix back into the canonical CIDR form. Both AF_INET
only — matching libbsd's inet_net_pton scope. Currently missing
entirely from frankenlibc-abi.

* core::inet::net_pton::parse handles dotted-decimal AND BIND's
  legacy "0xHHHH" hex form. Implicit prefix when no /N is present
  is octet_count * 8 ("192.168" → /16). Octet range [0, 255],
  prefix range [0, 32]. Strict on trailing garbage, double dots,
  >4 octets, missing-prefix-after-slash.
* core::inet::net_pton::format emits "192.168.0.1" for /32 with 4
  bytes (host shorthand) and "192.168.0/24" for partial networks
  — matching libresolv's elision rule.
* glibc_internal_abi shims publish both symbols at the libresolv
  names. Errno mapping: invalid → EINVAL, buffer too small →
  EMSGSIZE, non-AF_INET → EAFNOSUPPORT.

31 core unit tests covering: full host, every implicit prefix step
(/8, /16, /24, /32), explicit prefixes including /0 and partial-
octet (/20), hex form with and without prefix, every error class
(empty, octet overflow, too many octets, double dot, trailing dot,
prefix > 32, missing prefix, garbage after prefix, buffer too
small), and a round-trip property test. 12 abi integration tests
covering the errno mapping for each error category, NULL-src
guard, AF_INET6 rejection, and an end-to-end pton→ntop round
trip. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
